item.title: Honda slumps to first annual loss on $9bn EV charge
item.description: Japan’s second-biggest carmaker reports worse than expected result but forecasts return to profit this year

item.title: Mortgage refixing, longer lags and eggs
item.description: Plus, US oil producers aren’t drilling

item.title: FirstFT: Trump meets Xi for high-stakes summit
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Starmer faces leadership challenge and Warsh’s Fed confirmation

item.title: Private equity backers slam ‘runaway’ legal costs from top law firms
item.description: Body for pension and sovereign wealth funds wants costs shared between investors and buyout groups

item.title: Why Big Oil is opening new frontiers | FT Film
item.description: Suriname, South America’s smallest and greenest country, is tying its future to a $10.5bn offshore oil development led by TotalEnergies

item.title: UK manufacturers warn of ‘significant problems’ from steel tariff changes
item.description: Ministers should reconsider import measures due to take effect in July, says British Chambers of Commerce

item.title: The new circular trade in private credit
item.description: Private capital groups have a new opportunity to buy significant risk transfers tied to credit funds

item.title: China’s fuel exports fail to rebound after Beijing signals easing of ban
item.description: Data suggests shipments of refined fuels are not picking up in blow to Asian economies starved of supplies by Iran war

item.title: Walmart launches local US delivery hubs in quest for speed supremacy
item.description: Bringing groceries to customers within minutes is a competitive front in battle with ecommerce giant Amazon

item.title: UK’s financial ambulance-chasers are running out of road
item.description: The chances of claims management companies landing another big blow against the banks are fading

item.title: Self-report fraud and walk free, New York prosecutors tell Wall Street
item.description: SDNY attorneys that took on Drexel and SAC Capital adopt more lenient stance on corporate wrongdoing

item.title: Bank of England set to water down stablecoin rules after industry pressure
item.description: Deputy governor says initial plans may have been ‘overly conservative’ and central bank ‘looking very hard’ at alternatives

item.title: Ukraine arms maker plans satellite push to cut reliance on US
item.description: Fire Point is moving into space after developing drones and missiles, despite an ongoing probe for alleged graft

item.title: The luxury industry’s many contradictions
item.description: Exclusivity is not just a matter of selling some items that almost no one can afford

item.title: The risk of relying on EDF to deliver Europe’s nuclear renaissance
item.description: The group needs to cut costs and rejuvenate its reactor-building expertise

item.title: Will L&G be the City’s next domino to fall?
item.description: The FTSE 100 group’s chief executive says ‘this is our moment’ but private capital rivals are circling

item.title: Chinese demand spurs US ethane exports to record high
item.description: Iran war leads to shortage of petrochemical feedstock naphtha, prompting plastic makers to hunt for cheaper alternative

item.title: Tech groups score win on clean energy rules for gas-powered data centres
item.description: Corporate climate watchdog drops stricter proposal on net zero claims after heavy lobbying

item.title: Why Big Oil is opening new frontiers? | FT Film
item.description: Suriname, South America's smallest and greenest country, is tying its future to a $10.5bn offshore oil development led by TotalEnergies

item.title: GameStop’s Cohen threatens to take $56bn eBay offer to shareholders
item.description: CEO says reseller’s board failed to engage after declining to meet him

item.title: Cerebras boosts IPO price to raise $5.5bn
item.description: Chipmaker valued at $40bn in sign of high demand for AI stocks

item.title: Citadel tells key researchers to relocate from Hong Kong or quit
item.description: Hedge fund gives ultimatum to members of its global quantitative strategies team based in territory

item.title: UK estate agents at gloomiest in more than two years due to higher mortgage rates
item.description: Middle East conflict’s economic impact is hurting the housing market

item.title: Nationwide to crack down on payment message harassment
item.description: Ex-partners are subverting the ‘payment reference’ feature in bank transactions to send abusive messages

item.title: Elon Musk’s settlement with SEC raises ‘red flags’, judge says
item.description: Court refuses to ‘rubber stamp’ $1.5mn deal over Twitter stock disclosure

item.title: UK regulator pushes private credit groups to share more data
item.description: Fast-growing market comes under greater scrutiny for potential risks after series of setbacks

item.title: Xpeng in talks with VW about buying a factory in Europe
item.description: Carmaker dubbed ‘China’s Tesla’ also explores building a new plant on the continent

item.title: Shares surge in debut of power producer favoured by Trump energy agenda
item.description: Geothermal generator Fervo uses renewable energy technology that has garnered rare administration support

item.title: Italy’s Ludoil agrees to buy Beny Steinmetz-linked Sicilian oil refinery
item.description: Russia’s Lukoil sold the ISAB plant in 2022 to a Cypriot company backed by the convicted Israeli tycoon

item.title: Brown-Forman’s $17bn bid rejection leaves a bitter taste
item.description: The Brown family may have balked at handing its brands over to Sazerac, but it would have been more appealing than the alternatives
